entries.description: Check text against a brand voice profile using an LLM and score brand compliance.

schema.title: AI Brand Voice Check
schema.description: Check text against a brand voice profile using an LLM provider

toolMeta.description: Check text against a brand voice profile using an LLM provider

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description: API key for the AI provider

profileId.title: Profile ID
profileId.description: Brand voice profile to resolve from the store

doc.overview:
The **AI Brand Voice Check** tool evaluates each block's source text against a
brand voice profile using a configured LLM provider. The model returns
findings across voice dimensions — tone, style, clarity, and brand compliance —
each with a severity, message, and suggestion. The tool computes an overall
brand-compliance score, records the score and any findings on the block's
properties, and attaches a brand-voice annotation to the block. Blocks with
empty source text are skipped.

Provider credentials are required, since the tool makes an external API call per
block. The voice profile that defines the brand's guidelines is supplied by a
profile identifier resolved from the store, or programmatically by the host
(for example the brand command or a project's brand binding). This is the
LLM-based companion to the rule-based brand vocabulary check.

apiKey.description: API key for the AI provider.
apiKey.help:
Credential for the selected provider. Local providers such as Ollama may not
require a key. Prefer the credential store or environment over committing
keys to a recipe.

model.description: The model name to request from the provider.
model.help:
The provider-specific model identifier. When omitted, the provider's default
model is used.

profileId.description: Brand voice profile to resolve from the store.
profileId.help:
The identifier of the voice profile whose guidelines the text is checked
against. The profile may instead be supplied programmatically by the host,
in which case this field is not needed.

provider.description: The LLM provider backend to use.
provider.help: Selects which model backend performs the brand voice check.

limitations: Requires AI provider credentials; makes an external API call per block.
Without a resolved voice profile, the prompt has no brand guidelines to check against.
Evaluates source text; findings are model output and reflect the chosen model's judgement.
processingNotes: Operates on blocks; blocks with empty source text are skipped.
Blocks are processed in parallel by default for throughput.
A brand-voice score and findings are stored on the block, and a brand-voice annotation is attached.

entries.description: Detect named entities and terminology candidates in source content using an LLM.

schema.title: AI Entity Extract
schema.description: Detect named entities (people, organizations, products, locations) with an LLM, a local NER model, or both

toolMeta.description: Detect named entities (people, organizations, products, locations) with an LLM, a local NER model, or both

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description: API key for the AI provider

batchConcurrency.description: Number of concurrent batch calls (0 or 1 = sequential)

batchSize.description: Number of blocks per LLM call (0 or 1 = one block per call)

engine.title: Extraction Engine
engine.description: llm (AI provider; default) / ner (local on-device model — nothing leaves the machine) / hybrid (both)

ui:enum-descriptions.hybrid: Run the local model and the LLM, merging results.
ui:enum-descriptions.ner: On-device model — no credentials, nothing leaves the machine.

knownTerms.description: Terms to exclude from extraction (already in termbase)

doc.overview:
The **Entity Extract** tool analyses a block's source text with a large
language model and records two kinds of stand-off annotation: named entities
(people, organizations, products, locations, and also dates, times,
currencies, and measurements) and terminology candidates (domain-specific
terms that would benefit from a termbase entry). Each entity carries a
suggested do-not-translate flag; each term candidate carries a category and a
translatability classification (do-not-translate, consistent, or free). It is
read-only — it writes annotations only and never changes the source or target.

Extraction can optionally combine the LLM with a NER provider for fast entity
detection; the LLM classification is preferred where the two overlap. Blocks
can be analysed one at a time or grouped into batches sent in a single
structured call, and batches can run concurrently. Known terms already in the
termbase can be supplied so they are not re-proposed. A provider and, for
hosted providers, credentials are required.

apiKey.help:
Credential for the chosen hosted provider. Required for hosted providers;
not needed for a local Ollama backend.

batchConcurrency.description: Number of batch calls to run in parallel.
batchConcurrency.help:
How many batches are analysed concurrently. A value of 0 or 1 runs batches
sequentially.

batchSize.description: Number of blocks per LLM call.
batchSize.help:
Blocks are grouped into batches of this size and analysed in a single
structured call per batch. A value of 0 or 1 analyses one block per call.

knownTerms.description: Terms to exclude from extraction (already in the termbase).
knownTerms.help:
Terms the LLM is told not to re-propose, so the candidate list focuses on
genuinely new terminology. Matching is case-insensitive.

locale.help:
The locale of the text being analysed, passed to the LLM and any NER
provider so detection is language-aware.

model.help:
The provider-specific model identifier. When omitted, the provider's
default model is used.

limitations: Requires a provider and, for hosted providers, valid credentials; hosted providers make billed, rate-limited network calls.
The NER provider is optional and supplied programmatically; with no NER provider, extraction is LLM-only.
Entity and term suggestions (including the do-not-translate flag and translatability) are model proposals and should be reviewed before acting on them.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks with non-empty source; other parts pass through unchanged.
Read-only — writes entity and term-candidate annotations and never modifies source or target.
When both an LLM and a NER provider produce an entity at the same span, the LLM classification is kept.
Dates, times, currencies, and measurements are not defaulted to do-not-translate, since they need locale-specific formatting.

entries.description: Extract candidate terminology from source text using an LLM provider.

schema.title: AI Terminology Extraction
schema.description: Extract candidate terminology from content using an LLM provider

toolMeta.description: Extract candidate terminology from content using an LLM provider

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description: API key for the AI provider

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: Subject domain for terminology extraction (e.g. medical legal technology)

doc.overview:
The **Term Extract** tool sends each block's source text to a
configured LLM provider and asks it to return notable terms, each with a short
definition and domain. The extracted entries are recorded on the block's
properties as a terminology list. Blocks whose source is empty are skipped, and
blocks with no terms found are left unchanged.

Because the tool calls an external model, it requires provider credentials. An
optional subject domain can be supplied to bias extraction toward that field
(for example medical, legal, or technology terminology). The results are
candidate terms intended for review and curation into a termbase, not an
authoritative glossary.

apiKey.description: API key for the AI provider.
apiKey.help:
Credential for the selected provider. Local providers such as Ollama may
not require a key. Prefer supplying credentials through the credential store
or environment rather than committing them to a recipe.

domain.description: Subject domain to bias terminology extraction.
domain.help:
An optional hint naming the subject area (for example `medical`, `legal`, or
`technology`). When set, it is included in the prompt so the model favours
terms relevant to that domain.

model.description: The model name to request from the provider.
model.help:
The provider-specific model identifier. When omitted, the provider's
default model is used.

provider.description: The LLM provider backend to use.
provider.help: Selects which model backend performs the extraction.

limitations: Requires AI provider credentials and makes an external API call per block.
Extracted terms are model output and should be reviewed before being treated as authoritative.
Operates on source text only.
processingNotes: Operates on blocks; blocks with empty source text are skipped.
Blocks are processed in parallel by default for throughput.

examples.description: Bias extraction toward a particular subject area.

entries.description: Collect blocks into fixed-size batches before forwarding them downstream.

size.title: Batch Size
size.description: Number of blocks to collect before forwarding as a batch

doc.overview:
The **Batch Collector** buffers translatable blocks and forwards them in
groups of a configured size, instead of one at a time. It exists so that a
downstream tool which is cheaper to call in bulk — for example a batch
translation request — can receive several blocks together rather than block
by block.

Non-block parts (layer markers, data, media) pass through immediately and are
never buffered. Blocks accumulate until the batch reaches the configured size
and are then released in order; any remaining blocks are flushed when the
input stream ends. Stream order is preserved.

size.description: Number of blocks to collect before forwarding a batch.
size.help:
The tool buffers blocks until this many have accumulated, then forwards
them as a group. A larger size means fewer, larger handoffs to the next
tool; a size of `1` effectively forwards each block as soon as it arrives.

limitations: Only blocks are batched; all other part types pass through without buffering.
processingNotes: Remaining buffered blocks are flushed when the input stream closes.
Block order within and across batches is preserved.

examples.title: Collect blocks in groups of 50
examples.description: Buffer 50 blocks per handoff for a bulk-friendly downstream tool.

entries.description: Add or remove the Unicode byte-order mark (BOM) on document layers.

addBOM.title: Add BOM
addBOM.description: When true the Unicode BOM is added; when false it is removed

alsoNonUTF8.title: Remove UTF-16 BOMs Also
alsoNonUTF8.description: Also remove the BOM from UTF-16 files not just UTF-8

doc.overview:
The **BOM Convert** tool controls whether the output document carries a
Unicode byte-order mark. It operates on layer-start parts: when a layer
begins, the tool sets that layer's `HasBOM` flag, and the writer honours it
when serializing the document.

Use it to add a BOM where a downstream consumer expects one, or to strip a
BOM that would otherwise interfere with parsing. The tool changes only the
BOM flag on layers; it does not alter block text.

addBOM.description: Add the BOM when true; remove it when false.
addBOM.help:
When enabled, the document layer is marked to be written with a Unicode
BOM. When disabled, the layer is marked to be written without one.

alsoNonUTF8.description: Also handle the BOM on UTF-16 documents, not only UTF-8.
alsoNonUTF8.help:
By default the BOM handling targets UTF-8 documents. Enable this to also
apply the BOM setting to UTF-16 encoded files.

processingNotes: Operates on layer-start parts; the layer's BOM flag is set and the writer applies it.
Block, data, and media parts pass through unchanged.

examples.title: Remove the BOM
examples.description: Mark the document to be written without a byte-order mark.

examples.title: Add a BOM, including UTF-16
examples.description: Ensure a BOM is present on both UTF-8 and UTF-16 output.

entries.description: Flag forbidden, competitor, and preferred-term violations from a brand voice profile.

doc.overview:
The **Brand Vocabulary Check** tool scans each block's source text for
vocabulary violations defined by a brand voice profile. Forbidden terms are
flagged at major severity, competitor terms at critical severity, and where a
forbidden term has a preferred replacement, the finding includes that
suggestion. When a termbase is provided, the tool also looks up brand-vocabulary
terms in it, flagging competitor and forbidden entries it finds. Matches are
case-insensitive.

Findings are recorded on the block's properties and an overall brand-voice score
is attached as an annotation. This is a rule-based check that needs no LLM
credentials, and it is designed to run before the LLM-based brand voice check.
The voice profile and termbase are supplied programmatically rather than as
schema parameters; the profile can also be resolved lazily from an
organizational context hierarchy.
limitations: The voice profile and termbase are configured programmatically; they are not exposed as schema parameters.
Term matching is case-insensitive substring matching; it does not account for inflection or morphology.
Evaluates source text.
processingNotes: Operates on blocks; blocks with empty source text are skipped.
Without a resolved profile or termbase, no findings are produced.
Findings are stored on the block and a brand-voice annotation with an overall score is attached.

examples.title: Add to a flow
examples.description: The voice profile and termbase are supplied programmatically; reference the tool with an empty configuration.

entries.description: Change the letter case of source and/or target text to upper, lower, or title case.

doc.overview:
The **Case Transform** tool rewrites the case of text in translatable blocks.
It can uppercase, lowercase, or title-case the source text, the target text,
or both. It is useful for normalizing display strings — for example forcing
UI labels to upper case — or for preparing case-insensitive comparisons.

When applying to the target, a target locale must be set; the tool only
transforms locales that already have a target. Non-translatable blocks pass
through unchanged.

applySource.description: Apply the transformation to source text.
applySource.help: When enabled, the source text of each translatable block is transformed.

applyTarget.description: Apply the transformation to target text.
applyTarget.help:
When enabled, the target text is transformed for the configured target
locale. A target locale must be set; only existing targets are touched.

mode.help:
Selects the transformation. `upper` converts text to upper case, `lower`
to lower case, and `title` to title case (every letter uppercased, using
the Unicode title-case mapping).

limitations: Title case uses the Unicode title-case mapping applied to every letter, not word-initial capitalization.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks only; non-translatable blocks pass through unchanged.
Target transformation requires a target locale and only affects locales that already have a target.

examples.title: Uppercase source text
examples.description: Force all source strings to upper case.

examples.title: Lowercase the target translation
examples.description: Normalize target text to lower case for a locale.
examples.config:
mode: lower
applySource: false
applyTarget: true

entries.description: Count the characters in each block's source and target text, with and without spaces.

countInline.title: Count Inline Codes
countInline.description: Include inline code content in character counts

doc.overview:
The **Character Count** tool tallies the number of Unicode characters in every
translatable block. For each scope it records two figures: the total character
count and a count that excludes spaces. The results are stored on the block's
properties so downstream tools and reports can read them.

Counts are taken over the block's segmented text. By default both the source
and the active target locale are counted; the target is only counted when a
target locale is set and the block has a translation for it. Character counting
is useful for length budgeting (for example, UI strings with a fixed width) and
for character-based pricing.

countInline.description: Include inline-code content in the character counts.
countInline.help:
By default, inline codes — placeholders, variables, and tag content
represented as coded text — are **excluded** from the count, since they are
markup rather than translatable characters. Enable this only when you want
inline-code text reflected in the totals.

countSource.description: Count characters in the source text.
countSource.help:
When enabled, the characters in each block's **source** text are counted,
both including and excluding spaces. When neither source nor target is
explicitly requested, the tool counts both.

countTarget.description: Count characters in the target text.
countTarget.help:
When enabled, the characters in each block's **target** text are counted
for the active target locale, both including and excluding spaces. The
target count is only recorded when a target locale is configured and the
block has a translation for it.

limitations: Counting follows the active segmentation; results depend on how the text was segmented.
Target counts require a configured target locale and an existing translation for the block.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.
For each counted scope, both a total and a no-space character count are recorded on the block.

examples.title: Count source characters only
examples.description: Measure source-side character volume, ignoring any existing translations.

examples.title: Include inline codes
examples.description: Reflect placeholder and tag text in the character totals.

entries.description: Check target text for forbidden, missing, out-of-charset, or corrupted characters.

charset.title: Character Set Encoding
charset.description: Name of the character set encoding to check against (e.g. ISO-8859-1)

checkCharset.title: Check Against Charset Encoding
checkCharset.description: Warn if a character is not included in the specified character set encoding

checkCorrupted.title: Check Corrupted Characters
checkCorrupted.description: Check for common corruption patterns such as mojibake

forbiddenChars.title: Forbidden Characters
forbiddenChars.description: Characters that should not appear in target text (e.g. {}[])

requiredChars.title: Required Characters
requiredChars.description: Characters that must appear in target if present in source (e.g. punctuation)

doc.overview:
The **Characters Check** tool inspects each translation for character-level
problems and records findings on the block's properties as QA issues. It can
flag forbidden characters that must not appear in the target, required
characters that are present in the source but missing from the target, and
characters that cannot be encoded in a named character set. It also detects
common corruption: mojibake (UTF-8 misread as Latin-1), the Unicode replacement
character, and control characters. A target locale is required.

Corruption checking is on by default. The forbidden, required, and charset
checks are opt-in: the forbidden and required checks activate when their
character lists are supplied, and the charset check activates when explicitly
enabled.

charset.description: Name of the character set encoding to check against.
charset.help:
The IANA character set name used by the charset check (for example
`ISO-8859-1`). An unknown name produces a warning rather than failing the
run.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this to take effect

checkCharset.description: Check target characters against a named character set encoding.
checkCharset.help:
When enabled, every target character is tested for encodability in the
character set named by `charset`; characters that cannot be encoded are
flagged with a warning.

dependsOn.condition: names the character set used when this check is enabled

checkCorrupted.description: Check for common corruption patterns such as mojibake.
checkCorrupted.help:
When enabled, the target is scanned for sequences that indicate encoding
corruption — common mojibake pairs, the Unicode replacement character
(U+FFFD), and control characters other than tab, newline, and carriage
return.

forbiddenChars.description: Characters that must not appear in the target.
forbiddenChars.help:
A string whose characters are each treated as forbidden. The target is
flagged with an error for every forbidden character it contains. Leave empty
to skip this check.

requiredChars.description: Characters that must appear in the target if present in the source.
requiredChars.help:
A string whose characters must appear in the target whenever they appear in
the source. A warning is raised for each such character missing from the
target. Leave empty to skip this check.

limitations: Forbidden and required character lists are matched per character, not as substrings or patterns.
Corruption detection covers a fixed set of common mojibake patterns plus replacement and control characters.
Requires a configured target locale; blocks without a translation for it are skipped.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.
Findings are stored on the block as a JSON array of QA issues.

examples.title: Forbid specific characters
examples.description: Flag any target containing curly braces or square brackets.

examples.title: Restrict to a legacy encoding
examples.description: Warn about characters that cannot be encoded in ISO-8859-1.

entries.description: Collect the distinct characters used across source and target text, with their frequencies.

includeSource.title: Include Source
includeSource.description: Include source text in character listing

includeTarget.title: Include Target
includeTarget.description: Include target text in character listing

doc.overview:
The **Characters Listing** tool scans translatable blocks and accumulates the
set of distinct characters used, together with how often each one occurs. It
also records the number of unique characters per block on the block's
properties. The accumulated character set is typically used for font subsetting
— determining which glyphs a font must provide for a given body of content.

By default both source and target text are scanned. When target text is
included, a target locale must be supplied so the tool knows which translation
to read.

includeSource.description: Include source text when collecting characters.
includeSource.help:
When enabled, every character in each block's **source** text is added to
the character set and its frequency tally. At least one of source or target
must be included.

includeTarget.description: Include target text when collecting characters.
includeTarget.help:
When enabled, characters from each block's **target** text are added to the
set for the active target locale. A target locale is required when this is
enabled.

dependsOn.condition: requires a configured target locale when enabled

limitations: At least one of source or target inclusion must be enabled.
Including target text requires a configured target locale.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.
Frequencies are accumulated across all blocks; the per-block unique-character count is recorded on each block.

examples.title: List source characters only
examples.description: Build the character set from source text alone, for example before any translation exists.

entries.description: Flag common text-hygiene issues in source content (no target or locale comparison).

doc.overview:
The **Content Lint** tool inspects the source text of each translatable block
for common text-hygiene problems and records them as quality findings. It is a
generic, single-text checker: it looks only at the source and needs no target
or target locale.

It reports empty or whitespace-only content (a major issue), and the following
minor nits: leading or trailing whitespace, consecutive spaces, doubled words
(e.g. "the the"), and stray control characters. Clean content produces no
findings.
limitations: Inspects source content only; it does not compare source against a target.
Heuristic checks; it does not perform spelling or grammar analysis.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.
Findings accumulate under the unified quality findings annotation alongside other checkers.

examples.title: Lint source content
examples.description: Flag hygiene issues in the source text of every block.

entries.description: Create target segment containers for a locale, optionally seeded with the source text.

copySource.title: Copy Source
copySource.description: Copy the source runs into the new target

createOnNonTranslatable.title: Create on Non-Translatable
createOnNonTranslatable.description: Create a target even for non-translatable text units

overwrite.title: Overwrite Existing
overwrite.description: Overwrite the existing target if one already exists

doc.overview:
The **Create Target** tool ensures every block has a target segment for a
given locale. It is the standard first step when preparing content for
translation: it creates the empty (or source-seeded) target containers that
later tools — translation, leverage, QA — fill and check.

A target locale is required. By default existing targets are left untouched;
enable overwriting to replace them. Targets can be created for
non-translatable blocks as well, which keeps bilingual structure complete
across an entire document.

copySource.description: Seed the new target with the source text.
copySource.help:
When enabled, the source text is copied into the new target segment so it
starts as a duplicate of the source. When disabled, the target is created
empty.

createOnNonTranslatable.description: Also create targets on non-translatable blocks.
createOnNonTranslatable.help:
When enabled, target containers are created even for blocks marked
non-translatable, keeping the bilingual structure complete. Disable it to
skip non-translatable blocks entirely.

overwrite.help:
By default a block that already has a target for the locale is left
unchanged. Enable this to overwrite the existing target with a new
(empty or source-seeded) one.

targetLocale.description: The locale to create a target for.
targetLocale.help:
The BCP-47 locale identifier for the target to create (for example
`fr-FR`). On the CLI and in flows this is typically supplied by the
`--target-lang` value.

processingNotes: Existing targets are skipped unless overwrite is enabled.

examples.title: Create empty targets for a locale
examples.description: Prepare empty target containers ready for translation.

examples.title: Pre-fill targets with the source
examples.description: Seed targets with source text as a starting draft.

entries.description: Reuse translations from a previous document version for unchanged source blocks.

fuzzyMatch.title: Fuzzy Match
fuzzyMatch.description: Enable fuzzy matching for similar texts

doc.overview:
The **Diff Leverage** tool compares each block's source against the same
block in a previous version of the document and carries forward the previous
translation when the source is unchanged. It avoids re-translating content
that has not changed between versions, and tags each block with its diff
status — `new`, `unchanged`, `modified`, or `leveraged`. A target language is
required.

The previous version's source and target texts are supplied programmatically
(keyed by block ID); they are not part of the declarative schema. The two
schema-visible options control how the source comparison is performed.

caseSensitive.help:
When enabled, two sources must match exactly, including letter case, to
count as unchanged. When disabled, the comparison ignores case.

fuzzyMatch.description: Carry forward translations for closely similar sources.
fuzzyMatch.help:
When enabled, a changed source that is sufficiently similar to the
previous version (above an internal similarity threshold) still reuses the
previous translation and is tagged `leveraged` with a similarity score.
When disabled, only exact matches are reused.

limitations: Requires a target language and a previous-version corpus (supplied programmatically); the previous texts are not configurable via the schema.
Fuzzy matching uses a fixed internal similarity threshold.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks only; non-translatable blocks pass through unchanged.
Each block is tagged with a diff-status property; leveraged fuzzy matches also record a similarity score.

examples.title: Reuse exact matches only
examples.description: Carry forward translations where the source is identical.

examples.title: Allow fuzzy leverage
examples.description: Also reuse translations for closely similar sources.

entries.description: Verify that do-not-translate terms survive verbatim into the target.

caseInsensitive.title: Case-insensitive preservation
caseInsensitive.description: Accept a case-folded match in the target instead of requiring exact case

doc.overview:
The **Do-Not-Translate Check** tool guards terms that must reach the target
unchanged — product names, trademarks, code identifiers. For each configured
term that appears in the source as a whole word, it verifies the term is
present verbatim in the target; when it is missing, the block is flagged with a
critical finding (the "the AI translated your product name" case). It is
read-only: it annotates findings and never rewrites content.

The list of protected terms is supplied programmatically rather than as a
schema field — a project, recipe, or checkset provides them, and the termbase
can contribute more. A target locale is required; blocks without a target for
that locale are skipped. The only schema-level option controls whether a
case-folded match in the target is accepted.

caseInsensitive.description: Accept a case-folded match in the target instead of requiring exact case.
caseInsensitive.help:
Off by default, because do-not-translate is usually case-sensitive —
"iPhone" must not become "Iphone". When enabled, a case-insensitive match
in the target satisfies the check.

limitations: The list of protected terms is configured programmatically; it is not exposed as a schema parameter.
A term is only checked when it occurs in the source as a whole word; substrings of larger words are ignored.

When a term is absent from the target the finding records the verbatim term to preserve but proposes no automatic replacement — a miss needs a human or a re-translation, not a blind substring substitution.
Requires a configured target locale; blocks without a target for that locale are skipped.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks that have a target for the configured locale; other parts pass through unchanged.
Findings are emitted in the "do-not-translate" category at critical severity.

examples.title: Case-insensitive preservation
examples.description: Accept a case-folded match for protected terms in the target.

entries.description: Normalize text through a target character encoding and unescape escape sequences on input.

applySource.title: Apply to Source
applySource.description: Apply encoding conversion to source text

applyTarget.title: Apply to Target
applyTarget.description: Apply encoding conversion to target text

escapeAll.title: Escape All Extended Characters
escapeAll.description: Escape all extended (non-ASCII) characters in output

reportUnsupported.title: Report Unsupported Characters
reportUnsupported.description: Report characters not supported by the target encoding

targetEncoding.title: Target Encoding
targetEncoding.description: Target encoding name (e.g. utf-8 or iso-8859-1 or shift-jis)

unescapeCER.title: Unescape Character Entity References
unescapeCER.description: Unescape HTML character entity references (e.g. á) when reading input

unescapeJava.title: Unescape Java-style Notation
unescapeJava.description: Unescape Java-style \\uXXXX escape sequences when reading input

unescapeNCR.title: Unescape Numeric Character References
unescapeNCR.description: Unescape numeric character references (e.g. á) when reading input

doc.overview:
The **Encoding Convert** tool validates and normalizes block text against a
target character encoding. It round-trips the text — encoding to the target
charset and decoding back to UTF-8 — which surfaces and replaces characters
the target cannot represent. The chosen encoding name is recorded in a block
property so a downstream writer can emit the document in that encoding.

Before converting, the tool can decode escape sequences found in the input —
numeric character references, HTML character entity references, and
Java-style `\uXXXX` escapes — so that the real characters are evaluated
against the target encoding. A target encoding name is required, and a target
locale is required when applying to the target.

applySource.help: When enabled, the source text is normalized through the target encoding.

applyTarget.help:
When enabled, the target text is normalized through the target encoding
for the configured locale. Only existing targets are touched.

dependsOn.condition: required when applyTarget is enabled

escapeAll.description: Escape all extended (non-ASCII) characters in output.
escapeAll.help:
When enabled, extended (non-ASCII) characters are escaped in the output
rather than emitted directly.

reportUnsupported.description: Report characters the target encoding cannot represent.
reportUnsupported.help:
When enabled, characters that the target encoding does not support are
reported, so unmappable content can be reviewed.

targetEncoding.description: The character encoding to normalize text through.
targetEncoding.help:
An IANA encoding name such as `utf-8`, `iso-8859-1`, or `shift-jis`. The
text is encoded to this charset and decoded back to UTF-8, replacing any
characters the encoding cannot represent. The name is stored on the block
for downstream writers. Unknown encodings are rejected.

targetLocale.help:
The locale whose target text is converted. Required when `applyTarget` is
enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this to take effect

unescapeCER.description: Decode HTML character entity references on input.
unescapeCER.help:
When enabled, named HTML character entity references such as `á`
are decoded to their characters before conversion.

unescapeJava.description: Decode Java-style escape sequences on input.
unescapeJava.help:
When enabled, Java-style `\uXXXX` escape sequences are decoded to their
characters before conversion.

unescapeNCR.description: Decode numeric character references on input.
unescapeNCR.help:
When enabled, numeric character references such as `á` or `á`
are decoded to their characters before the text is converted.

limitations: Conversion validates representability by round-tripping through the encoding; characters the encoding cannot represent are replaced.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks only; non-translatable blocks pass through unchanged.
The target encoding name is written to a block property for downstream writers.

examples.title: Normalize targets to ISO-8859-1
examples.description: Validate target text against Latin-1 before writing.

examples.title: Decode entities, then normalize to Shift-JIS
examples.description: Unescape HTML entities in input and convert to a Japanese encoding.
examples.config:
targetEncoding: shift-jis
targetLocale: ja-JP
unescapeCER: true

entries.description: Classify the source text of each block as ASCII, UTF-8, or unknown.

doc.overview:
The **Encoding Detect** tool inspects the source text of every translatable
block and records its encoding characteristics on the block's properties: a
detected label (`ascii`, `utf-8`, or `unknown`) plus boolean flags for whether
the text is valid UTF-8 and whether it is ASCII-only. Text that is ASCII-only is
labelled `ascii`; otherwise valid UTF-8 is labelled `utf-8`; anything else is
`unknown`.

The tool takes no configuration. It is useful for spotting non-ASCII content
that may need particular care in downstream formats or fonts, and for
confirming that text is well-formed UTF-8.
limitations: Examines source text only; target translations are not classified.
Detection is limited to ASCII / UTF-8 validity, not a full charset-detection heuristic.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.

examples.title: Detect source encoding
examples.description: The tool takes no parameters; add it to a flow with an empty configuration.

entries.description: Run an external command-line program on block text and use its stdout as the result.

args.title: Arguments
args.description: Command arguments; use ${source} and ${target} as placeholders

sendAsStdin.title: Send as Stdin
sendAsStdin.description: Send text via stdin instead of command arguments

doc.overview:
The **External Command** tool pipes each block's text through an external
command-line program and captures the program's standard output as the new
text. It lets a pipeline reuse an existing CLI filter — a custom normalizer,
a spell-fixer, a transliterator — without writing a native tool.

Text can be sent to the command on standard input or substituted into the
argument list via the `${source}` and `${target}` placeholders. A command is
required, and a target locale is required when applying to the target. Each
invocation has a timeout; the exit code is recorded on the block, and a
non-zero exit leaves the text unchanged and records the error.

applySource.help: When enabled, the source text is sent through the command.

applyTarget.help:
When enabled, the target text is sent through the command for the
configured locale. Only existing targets are touched.

dependsOn.condition: required when applyTarget is enabled

args.help:
The argument list for the command. Use `${source}` and `${target}` as
placeholders; both are replaced with the text being processed.

command.help:
Path or name of the command-line program to execute for each block. Its
standard output replaces the block text.

sendAsStdin.description: Send the text on standard input rather than as an argument.
sendAsStdin.help:
When enabled, the text is written to the command's standard input. When
disabled, the text reaches the command only through the `${source}` /
`${target}` argument placeholders.

targetLocale.help: The locale whose target text is processed. Required when `applyTarget` is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this to take effect

timeout.help:
Maximum time to wait for the command to finish for a single block. The
command is cancelled when the timeout elapses.

limitations: A non-zero exit code leaves the text unchanged and records the exit code and error message as block properties.
The command runs once per block per scope (source and/or target), which can be slow for large documents.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks only; non-translatable blocks pass through unchanged.
Trailing newlines are trimmed from the captured output.

examples.title: Pipe target text through a formatter
examples.description: Run an external program on the target, sending text on stdin.
examples.config:
command: my-formatter
applyTarget: true
targetLocale: fr-FR

examples.title: Pass text as an argument
examples.description: Substitute the text into the argument list instead of stdin.
examples.config:
command: transform
args: ["--text", "${source}"]
applySource: true
sendAsStdin: false

entries.description: Convert between half-width and full-width Latin characters and punctuation for CJK content.

includeKatakana.title: Include Katakana
includeKatakana.description: Also convert Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation to half-width forms

includeLLS.title: Include Letter-Like Symbols
includeLLS.description: Also convert characters from the Letter-Like Symbols block to character sequences

includeSLA.title: Include Squared Latin Abbreviations
includeSLA.description: Also convert Squared Latin Abbreviations from the CJK Compatibility block to non-CJK character sequences

mode.title: Conversion Mode
mode.description: Conversion direction between half-width and full-width characters

doc.overview:
The **Full Width Convert** tool maps characters between their half-width and
full-width forms. In CJK text, ASCII letters, digits, and punctuation are
often written in full-width forms so they align visually with ideographs;
this tool converts in either direction. The core mapping covers ASCII
printable characters (`!`–`~`, full-width range `！`–`～`) and the space
(regular space ↔ ideographic space).

applySource.description: Apply the conversion to source text.
applySource.help: When enabled, the source text is converted.

applyTarget.description: Apply the conversion to target text.
applyTarget.help:
When enabled, the target text is converted for the configured locale.
Only existing targets are touched.

dependsOn.condition: required when applyTarget is enabled

includeKatakana.help:
When enabled, Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation are also
converted to their half-width forms.

includeLLS.help:
When enabled, characters from the Letter-Like Symbols block are also
converted to character sequences.

includeSLA.help:
When enabled, Squared Latin Abbreviations from the CJK Compatibility
block are also converted to non-CJK character sequences.

mode.help:
`to-half` converts full-width characters to their half-width (ASCII)
forms; `to-full` converts half-width characters to full-width forms.

targetLocale.help: The locale whose target text is converted. Required when `applyTarget` is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this to take effect

processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks only; non-translatable blocks pass through unchanged.
The core mapping covers ASCII printable characters and the space; the extended-block toggles add further character ranges.

examples.title: Convert full-width to half-width
examples.description: Normalize full-width Latin characters in the target to ASCII.
examples.config:
mode: to-half
applyTarget: true
targetLocale: ja-JP

examples.title: Convert half-width to full-width
examples.description: Render ASCII characters in their full-width forms.
examples.config:
mode: to-full
applyTarget: true
targetLocale: ja-JP

entries.description: Flag identical source strings translated differently, or different sources sharing one translation.

checkPerFile.title: Per-File Checking
checkPerFile.description: Process each input file individually instead of comparing segments across all documents

checkSourceInconsistency.title: Check Source Inconsistency
checkSourceInconsistency.description: Flag when different sources share the same translation

checkTargetInconsistency.title: Check Target Inconsistency
checkTargetInconsistency.description: Flag when the same source has different translations

displayOption.title: Inline Code Display
displayOption.description: Controls how inline codes are represented: original codes or generic markers or plain text

doc.overview:
The **Inconsistency Check** tool tracks the mapping between source text and its
translation across blocks and flags inconsistencies. By default it reports
target inconsistency: the same source string translated more than one way.
Optionally it can also report source inconsistency: distinct source strings that
share a single translation. Each block records a status (consistent or
inconsistent), the inconsistency type, and the alternative translations or
sources observed.

Source and target text are trimmed before comparison, and comparison can be
made case-insensitive. By default the tool compares segments across all input
documents; per-file mode resets its tracking state at the start of each document
so consistency is judged within a single file. A target locale is required.

caseSensitive.help:
When enabled, strings that differ only in letter case are treated as
distinct. Disable it to fold case differences together when judging
consistency.

checkPerFile.description: Judge consistency within each file rather than across all documents.
checkPerFile.help:
When enabled, the tool clears its source/target tracking at the start of
each document, so a string is only compared against others in the same file.
When disabled, comparison spans all input documents.

checkSourceInconsistency.description: Flag when different sources share the same translation.
checkSourceInconsistency.help:
When enabled, a block is flagged when its translation is also used for a
different source string. This catches cases where distinct meanings collapse
to one translation.

checkTargetInconsistency.description: Flag when the same source has different translations.
checkTargetInconsistency.help:
When enabled, a block is flagged when its source text has been translated
differently elsewhere. This is the primary inconsistency check.

displayOption.description: How inline codes are represented in the report.
displayOption.help:
Controls the rendering of inline codes when reporting alternatives:
`original` keeps the original code markup, `generic` uses numbered markers,
and `plain` strips codes to plain text.

limitations: Compares exact (trimmed) text; near-duplicate sources or translations are not detected.
Requires a configured target locale; blocks without a translation for it are skipped.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.
Tracking state is global by default and reset per document only when per-file checking is enabled.

examples.title: Check both directions of inconsistency
examples.description: Flag both differing translations of one source and shared translations of different sources.

examples.title: Per-file consistency
examples.description: Judge consistency within each document independently.

entries.description: Strip inline-code runs (placeholders and paired codes) from segments, leaving plain text.

includeNonTranslatable.title: Include Non-Translatable
includeNonTranslatable.description: Apply the removal action even to text units marked as non-translatable

replaceWithSpace.title: Replace With Space
replaceWithSpace.description: Replace line-break inline codes with spaces instead of removing them entirely

doc.overview:
The **Inline Codes Remove** tool removes inline-code runs — placeholders and
paired open/close codes — from segment content, leaving the surrounding plain
text. It is useful when a downstream consumer wants the textual content
without markup, for example to build a plain-text reference or to feed a tool
that cannot handle coded text.

Removal recurses into structured plural and select constructs so inline codes
inside them are also stripped. By default it applies to the target (a target
locale is required) and includes non-translatable blocks. Line-break codes
can optionally be replaced with a space so adjacent words do not run together.

applySource.description: Strip inline codes from source segments.
applySource.help: When enabled, inline-code runs are removed from the source segments.

applyTarget.description: Strip inline codes from target segments.
applyTarget.help:
When enabled, inline-code runs are removed from the target segments of the
configured locale.

dependsOn.condition: required when applyTarget is enabled

includeNonTranslatable.description: Also strip codes from non-translatable blocks.
includeNonTranslatable.help:
When enabled, the removal also applies to blocks marked non-translatable.
Disable it to leave non-translatable blocks untouched.

replaceWithSpace.description: Replace line-break codes with a space instead of removing them.
replaceWithSpace.help:
When enabled, line-break-style placeholder codes are replaced with a single space so that, for example, `foo
bar` becomes `foo bar` rather than `foobar`. Other inline codes are still removed entirely.

targetLocale.help: The locale whose target segments are processed. Required when `applyTarget` is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this to take effect

limitations: At least one of applySource or applyTarget must be enabled.
processingNotes: Removal recurses into plural and select constructs so codes inside them are stripped as well.
Paired open/close codes are dropped while their inner text content is retained.

examples.title: Strip codes from the target
examples.description: Produce plain target text for a locale.

examples.title: Keep word boundaries at line breaks
examples.description: Replace line-break codes with a space when stripping.
examples.config:
applyTarget: true
targetLocale: fr-FR
replaceWithSpace: true

entries.description: Apply format-specific tool chains to embedded child layers within a document.

doc.overview:
The **Layer Processor** intercepts embedded child layers — content of one
format nested inside another, such as HTML inside a JSON value — and runs a
format-specific tool chain over the parts of each child layer before emitting
them. It maps a child layer's format name to the chain of tools that should
process that layer's content.

When a child layer's format has no configured chain, its parts pass through
unchanged; non-child-layer parts always pass through. This tool is configured
programmatically: the pipeline-per-format mapping is supplied in Go code, so
it exposes no declarative parameters in flow or CLI configuration.
limitations: Configured programmatically only; the format-to-pipeline mapping is not expressible via flow or CLI config.
Only embedded child layers are intercepted; top-level layers and their parts pass through.
processingNotes: Parts of a child layer are buffered until the layer ends, then processed through the matching tool chain and re-emitted in order.
Child layers whose format has no configured pipeline pass through unchanged.

entries.description: Validate target length against absolute limits and source/target ratio thresholds.

checkMaxCharLength.title: Check Maximum Length Ratio
checkMaxCharLength.description: Warn if target exceeds a percentage of source character length

checkMinCharLength.title: Check Minimum Length Ratio
checkMinCharLength.description: Warn if target is shorter than a percentage of source character length

maxCharLengthAbove.title: Percentage for Long Text (Max)
maxCharLengthAbove.description: Max percentage of source length allowed for long text

maxCharLengthBelow.title: Percentage for Short Text (Max)
maxCharLengthBelow.description: Max percentage of source length allowed for short text

maxCharLengthBreak.title: Short/Long Threshold (Max)
maxCharLengthBreak.description: Character count threshold between short and long text for max check

maxChars.title: Maximum Characters
maxChars.description: Absolute maximum character count for target text (0 = disabled)

maxPercentage.title: Maximum Length Percentage
maxPercentage.description: Maximum target/source length ratio as percentage (0 = disabled)

maxWords.title: Maximum Words
maxWords.description: Maximum word count for target text (0 = disabled)

minCharLengthAbove.title: Percentage for Long Text (Min)
minCharLengthAbove.description: Min percentage of source length allowed for long text

minCharLengthBelow.title: Percentage for Short Text (Min)
minCharLengthBelow.description: Min percentage of source length allowed for short text

minCharLengthBreak.title: Short/Long Threshold (Min)
minCharLengthBreak.description: Character count threshold between short and long text for min check

minPercentage.title: Minimum Length Percentage
minPercentage.description: Minimum target/source length ratio as percentage (0 = disabled)

doc.overview:
The **Length Check** tool verifies that each translation stays within
configured length constraints and records any findings on the block's
properties as QA issues. It supports three kinds of limit: absolute caps on
character or word count; a simple maximum/minimum percentage of source length;
and a long/short ratio model that applies different percentage thresholds
depending on whether the source text is above or below a character breakpoint.
A target locale is required; blocks without a translation are skipped.

Each limit can be enabled independently, and any limit set to `0` is disabled.
The long/short ratio model mirrors the length-checker defaults used by the
Okapi Framework bridge, so its thresholds line up with the equivalent bridge
step.

checkMaxCharLength.description: Enable the maximum length-ratio check (long/short model).
checkMaxCharLength.help:
When enabled, the target is flagged if it exceeds a percentage of the source
length, where the percentage depends on whether the source is long or short.

checkMinCharLength.description: Enable the minimum length-ratio check (long/short model).
checkMinCharLength.help:
When enabled, the target is flagged if it falls below a percentage of the
source length, where the percentage depends on whether the source is long or
short.

maxCharLengthAbove.description: Maximum percentage of source length allowed for long text.
maxCharLengthAbove.help:
The maximum target/source ratio applied when the source is long (longer than
the maximum breakpoint).

maxCharLengthBelow.description: Maximum percentage of source length allowed for short text.
maxCharLengthBelow.help:
The maximum target/source ratio applied when the source is short (at or below
the maximum breakpoint). Short strings allow a higher ratio because small
length differences are proportionally large.

maxCharLengthBreak.description: Character threshold dividing short from long text (max check).
maxCharLengthBreak.help:
Source text longer than this many characters is treated as long for the
maximum-ratio check; shorter text is treated as short.

maxChars.description: Absolute maximum character count for the target.
maxChars.help:
Flags the target as an error when it exceeds this many characters. Set to
`0` to disable the absolute character cap.

maxPercentage.description: Maximum target/source length ratio, as a percentage.
maxPercentage.help:
Flags the target when its character length exceeds this percentage of the
source length. Set to `0` to disable.

maxWords.description: Absolute maximum word count for the target.
maxWords.help:
Flags the target as an error when it exceeds this many words. Set to `0` to
disable the absolute word cap.

minCharLengthAbove.description: Minimum percentage of source length required for long text.
minCharLengthAbove.help:
The minimum target/source ratio applied when the source is long (longer than
the minimum breakpoint).

minCharLengthBelow.description: Minimum percentage of source length required for short text.
minCharLengthBelow.help:
The minimum target/source ratio applied when the source is short (at or below
the minimum breakpoint).

minCharLengthBreak.description: Character threshold dividing short from long text (min check).
minCharLengthBreak.help:
Source text longer than this many characters is treated as long for the
minimum-ratio check; shorter text is treated as short.

minPercentage.description: Minimum target/source length ratio, as a percentage.
minPercentage.help:
Flags the target when its character length falls below this percentage of
the source length. Set to `0` to disable.

limitations: Percentage and ratio checks compare character lengths only when the source is non-empty.
Requires a configured target locale; blocks without a translation for it are skipped.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.
Findings are stored on the block as a JSON array of QA issues.

examples.title: Cap absolute character length
examples.description: Flag any translation longer than 80 characters, useful for fixed-width UI strings.

examples.title: Constrain target/source ratio
examples.description: Require the target to be between 30% and 200% of the source length.

entries.description: Normalize line endings in block text to LF, CRLF, or CR.

doc.overview:
The **Line Break Convert** tool normalizes line endings in block text to a
chosen style. It first collapses every line ending (`\r\n` and `\r`) to `\n`,
then rewrites them to the target style. Use it to make output consistent for
a platform that expects a particular convention — for example CRLF on
Windows-targeted files.

By default both the source and every target locale are converted.

applySource.help: When enabled, line endings in the source text are normalized.

applyTarget.help:
When enabled, line endings are normalized in the target text of every
target locale present on the block.

mode.help:
`lf` produces Unix line feeds (`\n`), `crlf` produces Windows carriage
return + line feed (`\r\n`), and `cr` produces classic carriage returns
(`\r`).

processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks only; non-translatable blocks pass through unchanged.
Target conversion applies to all target locales present, not a single configured locale.

examples.title: Convert to Windows line endings
examples.description: Rewrite all line endings to CRLF for source and targets.

examples.title: Normalize only the source
examples.description: Convert source line endings to LF, leaving targets untouched.

entries.description: Re-read low-confidence OCR/ASR lines with a configurable multimodal LLM

schema.title: Media Refine
schema.description: Re-read low-confidence OCR/ASR lines with a configurable multimodal LLM

toolMeta.description: Re-read low-confidence OCR/ASR lines with a configurable multimodal LLM

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description: API key for the AI provider

threshold.title: Confidence Threshold
threshold.description: Re-read extracted lines whose confidence is below this

entries.description: Verify that configured regex patterns are preserved or absent in target text.

doc.overview:
The **Pattern Check** tool validates target translations against a set of regex
rules and records findings on the block's properties as QA issues. Each rule
either requires a pattern to be preserved — the number of matches in the target
must equal the number in the source — or forbids a pattern from appearing in the
target at all. Preserved-pattern rules are typically used for placeholders,
variables, and format specifiers; forbidden-pattern rules catch markup or text
that must not survive into the translation.

A target locale is required. The set of patterns is supplied programmatically
rather than through the schema fields, so this tool is most often configured by
the host application or a flow definition that provides the rules.
limitations: The pattern rules themselves are configured programmatically; they are not exposed as schema parameters.
Requires a configured target locale; a block with no translation passes with no issues recorded.
A rule cannot both require and forbid the same pattern.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.
Preserved-pattern rules compare match counts between source and target; forbidden-pattern rules check for any match in the target.

examples.title: Add to a flow
examples.description: The pattern rules are supplied programmatically; reference the tool with an empty configuration and provide rules from the host.

entries.description: Verify interpolation placeholders and numbered tags are preserved in the target.

flagExtra.title: Flag extra placeholders
flagExtra.description: Report placeholders present in the target but absent from the source

doc.overview:
The **Placeholder Check** tool verifies that every interpolation placeholder
in the source survives, by count, into the target. A dropped `{count}` or an
unbalanced `<0>` is the kind of break that crashes a localized application at
runtime, so a placeholder present fewer times in the target than in the source
is flagged as critical. It is read-only: it annotates findings and never
rewrites content.

Placeholders are detected with a built-in pattern covering the common
interpolation styles — `{{name}}`, `${name}`, `%(name)s` (Python),
`%1$s` (positional), `%s` / `%d` / `%@` (printf / Objective-C),
`{name}` / `{0}` (ICU / .NET), and numbered tags such as `<0>…</0>`. A target
locale is required; blocks without a target for that locale are skipped.

flagExtra.description: Report placeholders present in the target but absent from the source.
flagExtra.help:
When enabled (the default), a placeholder that appears in the target but
not the source — usually a stray token — is reported as a major issue, in
addition to the always-critical report of placeholders dropped from the
target.

limitations: Detection is pattern-based and covers the common interpolation styles listed above; placeholder syntaxes outside that set are not recognised.
The check compares placeholder counts, not positions; a placeholder present the correct number of times but in the wrong order is not flagged.
Requires a configured target locale; blocks without a target for that locale are skipped.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks that have a target for the configured locale; other parts pass through unchanged.
Dropped placeholders are reported at critical severity; extra placeholders (when flagExtra is enabled) at major severity.

examples.title: Only report dropped placeholders
examples.description: Disable extra-placeholder reporting and flag only placeholders missing from the target.

entries.description: Set or modify key-value properties on blocks.

onlyTranslatable.title: Only Translatable
onlyTranslatable.description: Only set properties on translatable blocks

overwrite.title: Overwrite Existing
overwrite.description: Overwrite existing properties with the same key

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: Key-value pairs to set on each block

doc.overview:
The **Properties Set** tool writes a fixed set of key-value properties onto
blocks. Properties are metadata that travel with a block and can influence
downstream tools and writers. Use it to tag content — for example marking a
set of blocks with a workflow status or a routing hint.

At least one property must be configured. By default the tool overwrites
existing properties with the same key and applies only to translatable
blocks.

onlyTranslatable.description: Only set properties on translatable blocks.
onlyTranslatable.help:
When enabled, non-translatable blocks are skipped. Disable it to set
properties on every block regardless of translatability.

overwrite.description: Overwrite existing properties with the same key.
overwrite.help:
When enabled, a property whose key already exists on the block is
replaced. When disabled, existing properties are kept and only missing
keys are added.

properties.description: The key-value pairs to set on each block.
properties.help:
A map of property names to values. Every listed property is written onto
each processed block. At least one entry is required.
properties.values: object of string keys to string values (required)

examples.title: Tag blocks with a status
examples.description: Set a workflow property on every translatable block.

examples.title: Add metadata without overwriting
examples.description: Only add a key when the block does not already have it.

entries.description: Generate deterministic pseudo-translations for localization-readiness testing.

expansionPercent.title: Expansion Percent
expansionPercent.description: Extra padding percentage added to simulate translation expansion (0 = no padding)

prefix.title: Prefix
prefix.description: Characters prepended before each translated block

suffix.title: Suffix
suffix.description: Characters appended after each translated block

doc.overview:
The **Pseudo Translate** tool produces fake translations that exercise a
product's localization without a real translator. It replaces ASCII letters
with accented look-alikes, wraps each segment in marker characters, and can
pad the text to simulate the length growth of a real translation. The result
reveals hard-coded strings, clipped layouts, and encoding problems before
translation begins. A target language is required (the conventional pseudo
locale is `qps`).

Inline codes and placeholders are protected: text inside `{...}` markers and
non-text runs are passed through unchanged so the runtime can still substitute
parameters. The transform is deterministic, so the same input always yields
the same output.

expansionPercent.description: Extra padding to simulate translation expansion.
expansionPercent.help:
Adds padding characters proportional to the text length, so the output is
longer than the source — a common effect of real translation. A value of
0 adds no padding.

prefix.description: Characters prepended to each translated segment.
prefix.help:
A marker placed before the pseudo-translated text. The default shade
marker makes the segment boundary visible so untranslated, concatenated,
or clipped strings stand out.

suffix.description: Characters appended to each translated segment.
suffix.help: A marker placed after the pseudo-translated text, mirroring the prefix.

limitations: Only ASCII letters are accented; other characters and content inside `{...}` placeholder markers are passed through unchanged.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks with a non-empty source; other parts pass through unchanged.
Inline codes and placeholders are preserved; the prefix/suffix wrap the whole segment once.
With a session cache, already-pseudo-translated targets are reused and fresh results are written back.

examples.title: Standard pseudo-translation
examples.description: Pseudo-translate to the conventional qps locale.

examples.title: Simulate 30% expansion
examples.description: Pad output to surface layout clipping.

entries.description: Check translation quality with rule-based checks, or an LLM when a provider is set.

schema.title: Quality Check
schema.description: Check translation quality with deterministic rules or an LLM (select with --mode)

toolMeta.description: Check translation quality with deterministic rules or an LLM (select with --mode)

absoluteMaxCharLength.title: Absolute Maximum Characters
absoluteMaxCharLength.description: Maximum number of characters allowed in any target segment

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description: API key for the AI provider

checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.title: Check Absolute Maximum Length
checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.description: Flag target segments that exceed an absolute character count limit

checkCodeDifference.title: Check Code Differences
checkCodeDifference.description: Verify that target segments have the same inline codes as source segments

checkCorruptedCharacters.title: Check Corrupted Characters
checkCorruptedCharacters.description: Check for patterns indicating encoding corruption (e.g. UTF-8 opened as ISO-8859-1)

ui:enum-descriptions.rules: Local rule-based checks — no credentials, no network.

strictCodeOrder.description: Flag differences when codes appear in a different order between source and target

targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers.title: Include Number-Only Segments

doc.overview:
The **Quality Check** tool runs a battery of rule-based checks over each
translation and records the findings on the block's properties as a JSON array
of QA issues, along with a pass/fail flag. The checks span several categories:
general text issues (empty target, whitespace mismatches, double spaces,
doubled words, target identical to source, corrupted characters), inline-code
consistency, span constraint violations, regex pattern preservation, and length
ratios. A target locale is required; when a block has no translation, only the
empty-target check applies.

With no `--provider`, the checks are deterministic and need no credentials.
Setting `--provider` instead runs LLM-judged QA: the chosen model reviews each
translation for terminology, fluency, and accuracy. The rule-based toggles
below apply to the deterministic mode; `model` and `checks` apply to the LLM
mode. Every rule check can be toggled independently, and the length checks
share the long/short ratio model used by the dedicated length-check tool.

absoluteMaxCharLength.description: Maximum number of characters allowed in any target segment.
absoluteMaxCharLength.help: The absolute character cap used by the absolute-maximum-length check.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this limit to take effect

apiKey.description: API key for the LLM provider (LLM mode).
apiKey.help: Credential for the chosen hosted provider when `--provider` selects one.

checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.description: Flag target segments that exceed an absolute character count limit.
checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.help:
When enabled, the target is flagged when it exceeds the absolute character
count given by `absoluteMaxCharLength`, independent of source length.

dependsOn.condition: sets the limit applied when this check is enabled

checkCodeDifference.description: Verify that the target has the same inline codes as the source.
checkCodeDifference.help:
Flags warnings when inline codes are missing from or extra in the target
compared with the source, counted by code type.

checkCorruptedCharacters.description: Check for patterns indicating encoding corruption.
checkCorruptedCharacters.help:
Flags a warning when the target contains characters that suggest encoding
corruption (for example UTF-8 misread as ISO-8859-1, signalled by the
Unicode replacement character).

checkDoubleSpaces.description: Check for double spaces in the target.
checkDoubleSpaces.help: Flags a warning when the target contains two consecutive spaces.

checkDoubledWord.description: Check for consecutive repeated words in the target.
checkDoubledWord.help:
Flags a warning when the same word appears twice in a row in the target,
unless the word is listed in `doubledWordExceptions`.

checkEmptySource.description: Warn when the target is non-empty but the source is empty.
checkEmptySource.help: Flags a warning when the target has content but the source is empty.

checkEmptyTarget.description: Warn when the target is empty but the source has content.
checkEmptyTarget.help:
Flags an error when a block has source content but no (or empty) target
text. This is the one check that still applies when a block has no
translation at all.

checkLeadingWhitespace.description: Check for leading-whitespace mismatches between source and target.
checkLeadingWhitespace.help:
Flags a warning when the leading whitespace of the target differs from that
of the source.

checkMaxCharLength.description: Flag targets longer than a percentage of source character length.
checkMaxCharLength.help:
Enables the maximum length-ratio check, using a long/short model: the
allowed percentage depends on whether the source is above or below the
maximum breakpoint.

checkMinCharLength.description: Flag targets shorter than a percentage of source character length.
checkMinCharLength.help:
Enables the minimum length-ratio check, using a long/short model: the
required percentage depends on whether the source is above or below the
minimum breakpoint.

checkPatterns.description: Verify that source patterns have expected corresponding content in the target.
checkPatterns.help:
When enabled, configured source/target regex pattern pairs are checked so
that patterns found in the source are preserved in the target. The pattern
pairs themselves are supplied programmatically.

checkSpanConstraints.description: Check non-deletable and non-cloneable span constraint violations.
checkSpanConstraints.help:
Flags an error when a non-deletable inline code is missing from the target,
or a non-cloneable inline code is duplicated in the target, according to the
per-code constraints carried on the source runs.

checkTargetSameAsSource.description: Warn when the target is identical to the source.
checkTargetSameAsSource.help:
Flags a warning when the target text equals the source text and contains at
least one letter or digit. Number-only segments are governed by
`targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers`.

checkTerminology.help: Enables terminology verification within QA check. Disabled by default.

checkTrailingWhitespace.description: Check for trailing-whitespace mismatches between source and target.
checkTrailingWhitespace.help:
Flags a warning when the trailing whitespace of the target differs from that
of the source.

checks.description: Quality dimensions for the LLM judge (LLM mode).
checks.help:
The list of quality dimensions the LLM evaluates, e.g. terminology,
fluency, accuracy, consistency. Defaults to terminology, fluency, accuracy.

doubledWordExceptions.help:
A semicolon-separated list of words that may legitimately appear twice in a
row (for example certain pronouns), so they are not flagged by the
doubled-word check.

dependsOn.condition: only consulted when the doubled-word check is enabled

maxCharLengthAbove.description: Maximum allowed percentage of source length for long text.
maxCharLengthAbove.help: The maximum target/source ratio applied when the source is long.

maxCharLengthBelow.description: Maximum allowed percentage of source length for short text.
maxCharLengthBelow.help: The maximum target/source ratio applied when the source is short.

maxCharLengthBreak.description: Character count above which text is considered long (max check).
maxCharLengthBreak.help:
Source text longer than this is treated as long for the maximum-ratio check;
shorter text is treated as short.

minCharLengthAbove.description: Minimum required percentage of source length for long text.
minCharLengthAbove.help: The minimum target/source ratio applied when the source is long.

minCharLengthBelow.description: Minimum required percentage of source length for short text.
minCharLengthBelow.help: The minimum target/source ratio applied when the source is short.

minCharLengthBreak.description: Character count above which text is considered long (min check).
minCharLengthBreak.help:
Source text longer than this is treated as long for the minimum-ratio check;
shorter text is treated as short.

model.description: The model name to request (LLM mode).
model.help:
The provider-specific model identifier for LLM-judged QA. When omitted, the
provider's default model is used.

provider.description: LLM provider for AI-judged QA (omit for rule-based checks).
provider.help:
When set, QA runs as an LLM judge using this provider instead of the
deterministic rule checks. Options are populated from the provider
registry. Leave empty to run the rule-based checks with no credentials.

strictCodeOrder.description: Flag differences when codes appear in a different order.
strictCodeOrder.help:
When enabled, and no count differences were found, the target is flagged if
its inline codes appear in a different order than in the source.

dependsOn.condition: the order check runs alongside code-difference checking

targetSameAsSourceWithCodes.description: Include inline codes when comparing source and target for identity.
targetSameAsSourceWithCodes.help:
Controls whether inline codes are taken into account in the
same-as-source comparison.

targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers.description: Include number-only segments in the same-as-source comparison.
targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers.help:
When enabled, segments containing only numbers (and punctuation) are
included in the target-same-as-source check. Disable it to ignore
number-only segments, which are often legitimately identical.

limitations: Length and ratio checks compare character lengths only when source and target are both non-empty.
The regex pattern pairs used by `checkPatterns` are supplied programmatically, not as schema parameters.
Requires a configured target locale.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.
When a block has no translation, only the empty-target check applies.
Findings are stored on the block as a JSON array of QA issues with a pass/fail flag.

examples.title: Inline-code and whitespace checks only
examples.description: Focus on code preservation and whitespace, disabling the length-ratio checks.
examples.config:
checkCodeDifference: true
checkLeadingWhitespace: true
checkTrailingWhitespace: true
checkMaxCharLength: false
checkMinCharLength: false

examples.description: Flag any target segment longer than 200 characters.
